The largest faciolingual (mesiodistal) dimension for mandibular teeth tends to occur in the first molars due to their larger size, but among second molars, they generally have more extensive mesial and distal surfaces compared to first premolars. 

Answer Choice C: Lower 2nd molar Based on typical tooth morphology and cross-sectional dimensions, lower second molars exhibit the greatest width across the facial-lingual axis when compared to other listed options. This is primarily because these molars tend to be wider overall than upper molars or any type of premolar. Therefore, I select option C as my final answer.
